title: Atheropogon humilis
intro: Atheropogon humilis, also known as Atheropogon, is a plant species belonging to the family Poaceae, commonly found in New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, and is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Atheropogon humilis include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Atheropogon is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in the production of tinctures and topical creams.
taxonomy: Atheropogon humilis is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the true grass family. It is also classified as a member of the subfamily Pooideae. The genus Atheropogon is a group of small to medium-sized grasses native to Australia, with Atheropogon humilis being a species found in the southwestern region of the continent. More specifically, Atheropogon humilis is classified as a part of the tribe Poeae. The classification of Atheropogon humilis helps to understand its evolutionary relationships with other grass species.
